title: GI Bleeding
objectives: Understand the pathophysiology of GI Bleeding
Identify key diagnostic criteria for GI Bleeding
Recognize complications and when to escalate care

sections.title: Overview & Pathophysiology
sections.content: **GI Bleeding** — This section covers the fundamental pathophysiology, epidemiology, and clinical significance. Understanding the underlying mechanisms is essential for clinical reasoning and evidence-based management.

sections.title: Clinical Presentation & Diagnosis
sections.content: Key clinical features, diagnostic criteria, and workup for GI Bleeding. This section emphasizes pattern recognition and systematic diagnostic approaches.

sections.title: Management & Treatment
sections.content: Evidence-based management strategies for GI Bleeding, including first-line therapies, escalation pathways, and monitoring parameters.

sections.title: Complications & Follow-Up
sections.content: Potential complications, prognosis, and longitudinal management considerations for GI Bleeding.

keyTakeaways: Recognize the classic presentation of GI Bleeding
Identify red flags requiring urgent intervention

references.title: ACG Clinical Guideline: Upper GI Bleeding

reviewQuestions.question: Which of the following is the MOST important initial step in evaluating a patient with suspected GI Bleeding?
options: Obtain a thorough history and physical examination
Consult a specialist before any evaluation
reviewQuestions.explanation: A thorough history and physical examination is always the cornerstone of clinical evaluation for GI Bleeding, guiding subsequent diagnostic workup and management decisions.

reviewQuestions.question: What is the MOST critical factor in determining the management approach for GI Bleeding?
reviewQuestions.explanation: Clinical severity and appropriate risk stratification guide management decisions in GI Bleeding, determining the level of intervention and monitoring required.

reviewQuestions.question: Which complication of GI Bleeding requires the MOST urgent intervention?
reviewQuestions.explanation: Hemodynamic instability in the setting of GI Bleeding requires immediate intervention including fluid resuscitation, close monitoring, and potentially escalation to critical care.

imaging_reasoning.order_reasoning: Upper GI bleed → EGD within 24h; lower → colonoscopy; CTA if massive/unstable
imaging_reasoning.expected_findings: Active extravasation on CTA, bleeding source on endoscopy
imaging_reasoning.management_impact: Active bleed on CTA → IR embolization or surgery; stable → endoscopic intervention
